title: machine: Enable console autologin by default
body:
There are no security concerns here; this is a convenience for people debugging things.  Some podman-machine developers were manually setting a password over SSH for example, but this is just better than that.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

(Also marking no release note as we don't expose a console by default, only when launched with --debug right now)
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cgwalters*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Rebased.
It looks like the machine-(hyperv|mac) CI checks are broken generally?

OK rebased 🏄 - let's see what happens with the hyperv CI run on this.  I don't know what we do for a console on hyper actually, I'd expect this to be a no-op but perhaps it is somehow breaking something.

```
  > Enter [It] simple init with username - C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/init_test.go:124 @ 01/09/24 00:47:44.738
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --username remoteuser --image-path C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\podman_test872248153\fedora-coreos-39.20240104.2.0-hyperv.x86_64.vhdx e2d282390af6
  Error: EOF
  [FAILED] Expected
      <int>: 125
  to match exit code:
      <int>: 0
  In [It] at: C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/init_test.go:129 @ 01/09/24 00:48:15.905
```

Just a guess but you seem to hardcode the username and this test uses a different one so that might cause the failure? So I think agetty fails because you call it on a non existing user. That does however not explain why only hyperV fails but not qemu.

> Just a guess but you seem to hardcode the username and this test uses a different one so that might cause the failure? 

Thanks yep, that appears to be right.  @baude and I agreed for this use case it's simplest to just hardcode autologin to root (this is always a recovery scenario today anyways so might as well be root).
btw @Luap99 thanks for all the reviews and work you do!
Changed to just autologin as root since there's no reason not to, and it gets us out of interlock with the ignition user config.
